meta.title: G80 M3 Car Wash Plan
meta.car: Isle of Man Green BMW M3 G80 (ceramic coated)

ikFoamPro12.name: IK E Foam Pro 12
ikFoamPro12.note: Primary pre-soak sprayer. Used for Reset pre-foam, BH Touchless (maint. wash).

ikFoamPro2.name: IK E Foam Pro 2
ikFoamPro2.type: Electric battery-powered foam sprayer (dual mode — manual or electric)
ikFoamPro2.note: Battery-powered with built-in compressor. Also operable manually. Used for Koch Chemie MWC on wheels and engine bay APC.

ikFoam2Plus.name: IK Foam 2+
ikFoam2Plus.type: Manual pump sprayer — optional Xiaomi Mi Portable Electric Air Compressor 2
ikFoam2Plus.note: Primarily manual pump. Can be powered via Xiaomi Mi Portable Electric Air Compressor 2 for hands-free operation.

tools.name: IK E Foam Pro 12
tools.usedFor: Snow foam pre-soak — Reset; BH Touchless (maint.); HydrO2 Foam (maint.)

tools.name: IK E Foam Pro 2
tools.usedFor: Koch Chemie MWC on wheels; engine bay APC

tools.name: IK Foam 2+
tools.usedFor: Manual pump sprayer with optional Xiaomi Mi Portable Electric Air Compressor 2 — secondary foam tasks, spot treatment

tools.name: Xiaomi Mi Portable Electric Air Compressor 2
tools.usedFor: Powers the IK Foam 2+ for hands-free operation — alternative to manual pump

tools.name: Bucket 1 — WHEELS
tools.usedFor: Dedicated wheel wash — never crosses to paint

tools.name: Bucket 2 — WASH
tools.usedFor: CarPro Reset 1.35oz/5gal — paint mitt only

tools.name: Bucket 3 — RINSE
tools.usedFor: Clean water — rinse mitt after each panel

tools.name: Grit guards
tools.usedFor: One per bucket — mandatory on ceramic coat

tools.name: Microfiber wash mitt (long-pile)
tools.usedFor: Body panels only — never on wheels

tools.name: Dedicated wheel mitt / sponge
tools.usedFor: Wheel bucket only — separate from paint mitts

tools.name: EZ Detail Big Brush
tools.usedFor: Wheel face, spokes, outer barrel

tools.name: EZ Detail Little Brush
tools.usedFor: Tight spoke gaps, lug nut pockets

tools.name: Autofiber Barrel Blade
tools.usedFor: Inner barrel deep cleaning past lip

tools.name: Detail brushes (assorted)
tools.usedFor: Lug nuts, caliper fingers, brake dust crevices, trim gaps, engine bay

tools.name: Tire scrub brush (stiff)
tools.usedFor: Agitate ReTyre on sidewalls during pre-wash

tools.name: Foam tire applicator pad (Darkside)
tools.usedFor: CarPro Darkside — dedicated pads, never mixed

tools.name: Detail Factory Fender Well Brush
tools.usedFor: Wheel well agitation — AllClean or MultiX

tools.name: Pressure washer / hose w/ jet nozzle
tools.usedFor: Pre-rinse, all rinse passes

tools.name: Leaf blower
tools.usedFor: 100% touchless drying — paint, gaps, badges, exhausts; engine bay drying

tools.name: MF glass towels (exterior — dedicated)
tools.usedFor: Exterior glass — Bilt Hamber Trace-Less / Crystal only

tools.name: MF glass towels (interior — dedicated)
tools.usedFor: Interior glass — never mixed with exterior towels

tools.name: Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool (95160)
tools.usedFor: Interior windshield & rear screen

tools.name: Reach & Clean Tool — extra heads
tools.usedFor: Two-pass technique needs at least 2 heads per session

tools.name: Microfiber buffing towels
tools.usedFor: Spot correction, exhaust tips, trim work, engine bay wipe-down

tools.name: Soft trim brush
tools.usedFor: Exterior black trim dressing; engine bay plastic trim

tools.name: P21S applicator / soft MF cloth
tools.usedFor: Exhaust tips — P21S Polishing Soap

tools.name: Labeled spray bottles
tools.usedFor: Iron X, APC, Bug Out, Tar X, Trace-Less / Crystal, QD

tools.name: Measuring cup / graduated cylinder
tools.usedFor: Accurate dilution of Reset, Descale, BH Touchless

tools.name: Foam or MF applicator pads (Descale)
tools.usedFor: Applying Descale 1:100 by hand — panel by panel

tools.name: Plastic bags
tools.usedFor: Covering alternator, fuse box, intake, connectors before wet wash

tools.name: Painter's tape
tools.usedFor: Securing bags over sensitive components

tools.name: Nitrile gloves
tools.usedFor: Hand protection during all chemical stages — Iron X, Tar X, Bug Out, APC, ReTyre, Descale, and engine bay work. Change between stages when switching between strong chemicals.

tools.name: Safety glasses / eye protection
tools.usedFor: Eye protection during all spray chemical stages — Iron X, Tar X, Bug Out, ReTyre, APC, Descale, and engine bay work. Essential when using IK foam sprayers overhead.

tools.name: Bissell 3624 Spot Clean Professional
tools.usedFor: Carpet and fabric extraction — used with Adam's Polishes Carpet Cleaner for carpet deep cleaning and TuxMat cleaning

chemicals.name: CarPro Reset
chemicals.usedOn: All painted panels — pH neutral, ceramic-safe

dilutions.context: IK E Foam Pro 12 pre-foam (6L fill)
dilutions.note: CarPro does not publish a specific IK foam sprayer ratio. 1:50 is a practical starting point — adjust for desired foam density. Do not allow to dry on surface.
Fill bucket as water runs in to activate foam. 4 capfuls = 1oz. Wash top down, out of direct sun. Rinse mitt in second bucket periodically. Do not allow to dry on surface.

chemicals.tool: IK E Foam Pro 12 pre-foam + 2-bucket wash (Buckets 2 & 3)

chemicals.name: CarPro Iron X
chemicals.usedOn: All painted panels

dilutions.note: Acid free, pH neutral (6–7). Sprays on transparent, turns purple/red as it bonds to iron particles. Do not let dry in direct sun. Rinse thoroughly. Safe on clear coat paint.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle

chemicals.name: CarPro Bug Out
chemicals.usedOn: Front bumper, hood edge, lower windshield, mirrors — dry surface

dilutions.note: Spray on insect remains. Wait 30 sec–2 min depending on environment (5–30 sec on sensitive surfaces). Agitate as needed with MF towel. Remove and neutralise residue with CarPro ECH2O or Reset. Do not leave on surface more than 5 min. Avoid direct sun. Do not apply to hot surfaces.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle + dedicated bug MF towel

chemicals.name: CarPro Tar X
chemicals.usedOn: Lower rockers, rear bumper, door bottoms — DRY surface only

dilutions.note: Spray on dry cool surface. Dwell 2-3 min (max 7-10 min). Wipe with dedicated MF. Wash area with shampoo after.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle + dedicated tar MF towel

chemicals.name: Koch Chemie Magic Wheel Cleaner (MWC)
chemicals.usedOn: All wheel surfaces — iron-reactive, acid-free, viscous cling

dilutions.amount: No dilution — shake briefly before use
dilutions.note: Do not use on hot paint or surfaces or in direct sunlight. Check suitability and compatibility before use. Shake briefly. Spray rim surface and rim well thoroughly. Depending on dirt level, allow 2–5 minutes for MWC to take effect. Rinse thoroughly with high-pressure spray.

chemicals.tool: IK E Foam Pro 2 or spray direct; EZ Detail brushes + Barrel Blade; high-pressure rinse

chemicals.name: Brake Buster
chemicals.usedOn: Heavily contaminated wheels — alternative to MWC

dilutions.note: Use when wheels are unusually contaminated, e.g. after track days.

chemicals.tool: Spray direct + EZ Detail brushes + Barrel Blade

chemicals.name: DIY Detail AllClean (APC)
chemicals.usedOn: Wheel wells; door jambs (separate bottle); engine bay

dilutions.note: Stronger dilution for jamb grime accumulation

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle + Detail Factory Fender Well Brush (wells); detail brushes (engine bay)

chemicals.name: CarPro MultiX (APC option)
chemicals.usedOn: Wheel wells, door jambs, engine bay — alt. to AllClean

dilutions.note: Spray, foam, or saturate surface. Agitate as needed. Rinse thoroughly. Test on inconspicuous area before each use. Do not use on hot surfaces or in direct sun. Do not use on anodized aluminum.
For use in pressure washing applications. Test on inconspicuous area first.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle + Detail Factory Fender Well Brush / detail brushes

chemicals.name: Bilt Hamber Touchless
chemicals.usedOn: Full exterior — touchless alkaline pre-soak before Descale

dilutions.context: IK E Foam Pro 12 / pump spray / trigger — per bilthamber.com
dilutions.amount: Minimum 0.5% PIR for light cleaning, up to 2% PIR for very dirty vehicles. One-time calibration required: fill IK E Foam Pro 12 with 6L water, discharge into bucket, measure total output. Multiply output by 0.005–0.02 to get ml of Touchless needed.
dilutions.note: Apply to cool panels in shaded conditions. Do not allow to dry in use. Power-wash or rinse off completely. 1 litre of diluted product should last 4–5 washes. Contains corrosion inhibitors. Sugar-based, biodegradable, eco-friendly — no phosphates, solvents, NTA, or caustic soda.

chemicals.tool: IK E Foam Pro 12 (primary); also compatible with pump spray or trigger spray per BH

chemicals.name: CarPro Descale
chemicals.usedOn: Full exterior — mineral deposits & water spot etching in coating — panel by panel

dilutions.context: IK E Foam Pro 12 or spray bottle — per carpro-us.com
dilutions.note: For light contamination: apply as snow foam, allow maximum dwell without drying, rinse, then hand wash. Do not allow to dry on surface. Test on inconspicuous area before use. Not for regular weekly washing — use Reset for that.
For heavier contamination: wash panel by panel starting with horizontal panels. Allow to dwell as long as possible without drying. Repeat if necessary. Rinse thoroughly.

chemicals.tool: 2-bucket method — dedicated Descale wash bucket + rinse bucket

chemicals.name: CarPro ReTyre
chemicals.usedOn: Tire sidewall — pre-wash stage only. Not repeated before dressing.

dilutions.amount: No dilution — high pH foaming formula
dilutions.note: Use PPE — lung, eye, and skin protection required. High pH chemistry strips dressings and browning from rubber pores. Foaming design keeps product where applied. If ReTyre drips onto wheel surfaces, dilute with water and rinse immediately. Do not use on anodized wheels, matte/satin finishes, or in direct sun/on hot surfaces. Surface must be fully rinsed and 100% dry before applying DarkSide.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle with foaming nozzle + stiff tire brush

chemicals.name: CarPro Darkside
chemicals.usedOn: Tire sidewall only — standalone durable sealant, no base layer required

dilutions.amount: No dilution — shake well before use
dilutions.note: Surface must be 100% dry and clean (use ReTyre or MultiX first). Squirt onto foam or MF applicator — or Darkside Applicator Brush. Spread evenly across sidewall. Wipe off any excess. Allow surface to dry before vehicle is used — MINIMUM 1–2 hours dry time. Contains citral — may cause allergic reaction. Use in ventilated area. Avoid skin and eye contact. Does not sling when applied as directed.

chemicals.tool: Dedicated foam tire applicator pads (Darkside-only, never mixed)

chemicals.name: CarPro Reload 2.0
chemicals.category: Paint sealant / coating topper — both modes
chemicals.usedOn: All painted panels after blower drying — silica-based spray sealant, up to 3 months protection. Safe on wet or dry paint, rubber, and plastic trim. Do not apply to glass.

dilutions.amount: 3–4 sprays onto MF towel OR 1–2 sprays directly on panel — shake well first
dilutions.note: Apply wet or dry. IMMEDIATELY spread evenly over a 2–2.5 sq ft section. Work into surface until almost flashed. Flip towel and buff in further. Switch to a fresh clean MF towel and buff off completely. Check each panel before moving to next. Do NOT apply in direct sunlight. Excellent on rubber and plastic trim — does not stain. If uneven or hazy after buffing, wipe off with IPA immediately and re-apply. Up to 3 months protection on daily drivers.

chemicals.tool: 2 dedicated MF towels per session — one to spread, one to buff off. Never use on glass.

chemicals.name: P21S Polishing Soap
chemicals.usedOn: Exhaust tips & tailpipes — carbon soot and oxidation removal

dilutions.note: Circular motion on each exhaust tip. Wipe clean. Keep soap off surrounding painted bumper.

chemicals.tool: P21S applicator or soft MF cloth

chemicals.name: Bilt Hamber Trace-Less
chemicals.usedOn: All exterior & interior glass including windshield and rear screen

dilutions.note: Spray directly onto pane. Overlapping strokes with dedicated exterior MF glass towels. Second dry pass if haze remains.
Spray onto MF head of Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool — NOT directly onto glass. First pass damp to clean; swap to fresh head for dry buff pass.

chemicals.tool: Dedicated exterior MF glass towels; Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool (interior)

chemicals.name: Plastic / rubber trim dressing
chemicals.usedOn: All exterior black trim, door seals, weatherstripping; engine bay plastics and hoses

dilutions.note: Exterior: soft trim brush or foam pad. Engine bay: apply thin — do not over-saturate near hot surfaces or connectors.

chemicals.tool: Soft trim brush or foam applicator pad

chemicals.name: QD / spot detailer
chemicals.usedOn: Final inspection pass — spot correction of water marks, fingerprints, missed areas

dilutions.note: Recommended: CarPro Reload or Gyeon Q²M Cure — both coating-safe.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle + clean MF towel

chemicals.name: Bilt Hamber Touch-On
chemicals.usedOn: Full exterior painted surfaces, glass, plastics, metals — durable hydrophobic ceramic protection treatment. Also acts as a low-foaming shampoo for the bucket wash stage. Designed to be used after BH Touch-Less pre-wash.

dilutions.context: Pressure washer / lance reservoir — per bilthamber.com
dilutions.amount: Add 30ml into washer or lance reservoir, top up to 300ml with clean cool water. Shake well.
dilutions.note: Apply under pressure to entire vehicle. Immediately rinse using pressure washer. Dry with MF cloth or air blower. Vehicle must be cool and out of direct sunlight. Pre-wash with Touch-Less first.

dilutions.amount: Pour 30–60ml into bucket, fill with 10L warm water from fast running tap. Mix well.
dilutions.note: Apply using noodle wash mitt, sponge or similar. Rinse with clean water. Dry with MF cloth. Not a high foaming bucket wash.

dilutions.amount: Add 30ml to trigger bottle, top up to 1L with demineralised/deionised water. Shake well.
dilutions.note: Trigger apply liberally panel by panel, then thoroughly rinse with pressure washer or hose. Dry with MF towel or air blower. Car must NOT be allowed to air dry — water spotting may occur.

chemicals.tool: Pressure washer lance / trigger spray / bucket + wash mitt — one 30ml sachet per session

chemicals.name: Bilt Hamber Korrosol
chemicals.usedOn: All painted panels — removes embedded airborne metallic particles and brake dust from paintwork and lacquer. Non-acid, non-alkaline. Alternative/complement to CarPro Iron X.

dilutions.amount: No dilution — supplied with trigger spray
dilutions.note: Non-acid, non-alkaline colour-changing fallout remover for paintwork. Spray onto panel — formula turns purple/red as it reacts with embedded metallic particles. Rinse off with hose or jetwash. Use every 6–12 months or when contamination is felt with fingertips. Designed specifically for paintwork (not wheels — use Auto-Wheel for wheels). No agitation required in most cases.

chemicals.tool: Trigger spray direct — no agitation required in most cases

chemicals.name: Bilt Hamber Surfex HD
chemicals.usedOn: Wheel wells, door jambs, engine bay, tyres, brake calipers, interior plastics — versatile APC covering exterior and interior surfaces. Alternative/complement to AllClean and MultiX.

dilutions.note: Apply with sponge, saturate area, extract with wet vac while wet. Repeat with made-up solution to remove dirt.

dilutions.context: Interior headlining / sun visors — per bilthamber.com
dilutions.note: Best product for light coloured headlining and sun visors per BH.

dilutions.context: Interior plastic — lower door cards / plastics — per bilthamber.com
dilutions.note: Per BH FAQ: best cleaner for lower door cards and interior plastics.

dilutions.context: Wheel arches / wheel wells / tyres — per bilthamber.com
dilutions.note: Saturate cool surface, dwell 1–5 min, agitate heavy soil with stiff bristled brush, rinse with clean cold water.
Per BH: safe on rubber seals at this dilution.

dilutions.context: Engine bay / heavy grease and oil — per bilthamber.com
dilutions.amount: Use concentrated for maximum grease/oil removal
dilutions.note: Can be used from 1% (light) up to 100% neat for heavy industrial degreasing. Always spot test before use.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle + appropriate brush (stiff for exterior/engine, soft for interior); IK E Foam Pro 2 for larger areas

chemicals.name: Bilt Hamber Auto Wheel
chemicals.usedOn: Alloy and steel wheel faces — non-acid, non-alkaline iron-reactive wheel cleaner. Alternative/complement to Koch Chemie MWC and Brake Buster.

dilutions.note: Spray Auto-Wheel liberally onto wheels. TIP: Avoid directly spraying centre caps — spray radially outward from centre. Wait for colour-changing reaction, normally 1–5 minutes depending on contamination level. Wash wheel thoroughly with hose or jetwash. In severe cases use included/dedicated wheel brush to assist. Not required every wash — use when brake dust contamination is observed, typically every 4 months. Non-acid, non-alkaline.

chemicals.tool: Trigger spray direct; EZ Detail brushes + Barrel Blade for agitation if needed; hose/jetwash to rinse

chemicals.name: Koch Chemie Speed Glass (GC)
chemicals.usedOn: iDrive curved display and instrument cluster screen only — alcohol-free, anti-static

dilutions.context: All glass and smooth surfaces — per kcxusa.com
dilutions.amount: No dilution — spray on undiluted and wipe off
dilutions.note: Ready-to-use. Spray on undiluted and wipe off. Repeat as needed. Runs off quickly without streaks. Gloss and smoothness additive improves wiping effect of cleaning cloth. Safe on interior and exterior glass, screens, piano black, and glossy surfaces. Fresh fragrance.

chemicals.tool: Spray onto dedicated screen MF cloth or glass MF towel — wipe and buff

chemicals.name: Autofinesse Crystal
chemicals.category: Glass cleaner — alternative to BH Trace-Less
chemicals.usedOn: All exterior and interior glass, tinted windows, polycarbonate/acrylic surfaces. Safe on ceramic-coated glass. Use interchangeably with BH Trace-Less.

dilutions.amount: Light mist — ~3 sprays per panel, or spray onto dedicated MF glass towel
dilutions.note: Spray light mist directly onto glass OR onto cloth. Wipe in straight lines with light pressure — horizontal outside, vertical inside to avoid missing spots. Buff immediately with dry side of towel. Do not apply in direct sunlight or extreme temperatures. Alcohol-based with balanced distilled solvents. Safe on ceramic coated glass — will not degrade coating. Also works on wiper blades: spray directly onto blade and wipe with clean MF cloth.

dilutions.context: Interior glass — incl. windshield via Reach & Clean Tool — per autofinesse.com
dilutions.amount: Light spritz onto MF cloth — NOT directly onto glass where possible
dilutions.note: For lightly soiled interior glass, spray onto cloth and buff. For heavier interior haze, spray directly onto glass and wipe. Use with Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool for windshield. Best results with dedicated waffle-weave MF glass towel.

chemicals.tool: Dedicated MF glass towels (waffle-weave preferred) — separate exterior and interior towels. Interior: Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool.

normal.title: Park in shade — cool panels
normal.desc: Never wash a hot car or in direct sunlight. Ceramic coating accelerates flash-drying on ION Green metallic. All chemical applications require cool surfaces.

normal.title: Spot-treat bugs — CarPro Bug Out
normal.desc: Spray Bug Out on front bumper, hood edge, lower windshield, and mirrors on DRY surface. Dwell 5–30 sec (up to 2 min max for baked-on). Agitate gently with soft MF. Neutralise residue with Reset or ECH2O. Do not leave more than 5 min. Proceed to Tar X before any rinsing.

normal.title: Spot-treat tar — CarPro Tar X
normal.desc: Spray Tar X on lower rockers, rear bumper, door bottoms on DRY cool surface. Dwell 2–3 min (max 7–10 min). Wipe residue with dedicated tar MF. Wash treated area with shampoo after.

normal.title: Pre-rinse wheels & wheel wells
normal.desc: Blast loose brake dust and debris from all four wheels and wells before chemical contact. M compound brakes shed heavy iron.

normal.title: Apply Koch Chemie MWC to wheels
normal.desc: Load MWC RTU into IK E Foam Pro 2 (1.25L) undiluted or spray direct. Dwell 2–5 min until red iron-bleed visible. Agitate face/spokes with EZ Detail Big Brush, inner barrel with Autofiber Barrel Blade, tight gaps with Little Brush and detail brushes. Rinse thoroughly. Use Brake Buster if heavily contaminated.

normal.title: Clean tire sidewalls — CarPro ReTyre
normal.desc: Rinse tire first. Spray ReTyre RTU with foaming nozzle ONLY. Dwell 30 sec. Agitate with stiff tire brush. Rinse completely. If ReTyre contacts wheel face, dilute and rinse immediately. This is the ONLY ReTyre step — not repeated before dressing.

normal.title: Agitate wheel wells — APC
normal.desc: Spray AllClean (5:1–10:1) or MultiX (10:1) into wheel wells. Agitate with Detail Factory Fender Well Brush. Rinse.

normal.title: Clean door jambs — APC (optional)
normal.desc: Optional — every 3–4 washes or when visibly dirty. Work one door at a time, door fully open. Pre-rinse with low-pressure hose top to bottom, flushing drain holes. Spray AllClean (3:1–5:1) or MultiX (10:1) into jamb, dwell 1–2 min. Agitate flat surfaces with a soft detail brush, hinge hardware and striker plate with a stiff brush, and rubber seals thoroughly. Rinse completely top to bottom — ensure all APC is flushed from drain holes or it will streak down paint when door opens. Blow dry with leaf blower into hinge recesses and drain holes, follow with MF towel on flat surfaces. Dress rubber seals with trim dressing after drying.

normal.title: Pre-rinse full car
normal.desc: Rinse the entire car top to bottom with a hose or pressure washer before applying Iron X. This removes loose surface contamination and ensures panels are wet, which helps Iron X spread evenly and prevents it from flashing dry on the ceramic coating.

normal.title: Apply CarPro Iron X to paint
normal.desc: Spray Iron X RTU across all painted panels. Dwell 3–5 min — purple bleed indicates iron in coating. Do NOT let dry on ceramic. Rinse completely.

normal.title: Snow foam pre-soak — CarPro Reset
normal.desc: Add approx. 120ml (4oz) Reset to IK E Foam Pro 12, fill to 6L mark with water (~1:50). Apply thick foam top to bottom, dwell 5 min. Do not let dry. Rinse off completely before contact wash. NOTE: CarPro's published 1:9 ratio is for pressure washer foam cannons only — not applicable here.
chems: CarPro Reset — ~120ml per 6L (~1:50 in IK E Foam Pro 12)

normal.title: Two-bucket contact wash — top to bottom
normal.desc: Fill Bucket 2 (WASH) with 5 gal water + 1.35oz (40ml) Reset (1:500). Work one panel at a time, top to bottom. Rinse mitt in Bucket 3 (RINSE) after each panel, drag across grit guard before reloading. Light pressure only. Never use paint mitts on wheels.

normal.title: Final sheeting rinse
normal.desc: Remove nozzle tip and flood panels with a flat sheet of water top to bottom. Ceramic beading reduces standing water volume before blower drying.

normal.title: Touchless leaf blower drying — paint
normal.desc: Blow all paint surfaces dry: roof → hood → trunk → sides → lower panels. Chase water from mirror housings, door jambs, badges, trim gaps, exhaust outlets. ZERO towel contact on paint.

normal.title: Apply CarPro Reload 2.0 — paint sealant
normal.desc: Apply after paint is fully blower-dried. Work one panel at a time. Shake well. Spray 3–4 sprays onto a dedicated MF towel (or 1–2 sprays directly on panel). Spread evenly immediately. Wait 30–60 sec until almost flashed. Flip towel and buff in. Switch to a second clean dry MF towel and buff off completely. Do NOT apply in direct sunlight. Do NOT apply to glass. If uneven or hazy after buffing, wipe off with IPA and re-apply.
chems: CarPro Reload 2.0 — RTU, 3–4 sprays per section onto towel

normal.title: Clean exhaust tips — P21S Polishing Soap
normal.desc: Apply small amount of P21S direct to soft MF cloth or applicator. Circular motions on each exhaust tip to cut carbon soot and oxidation. Wipe clean. Keep soap off surrounding painted bumper.
chems: P21S Polishing Soap — small amount direct

normal.title: Clean exterior glass — BH Trace-Less or Autofinesse Crystal
normal.desc: Use either BH Trace-Less or Autofinesse Crystal — both RTU, both safe on ceramic-coated glass. BH Trace-Less: shake well, atomise fine mist on glass, wipe with waffle-weave MF turning cloth regularly, buff dry with suede MF. Autofinesse Crystal: spray light mist directly on glass or onto cloth, wipe in straight lines (horizontal outside), buff with dry side of towel immediately. Do not apply in direct sunlight. Use dedicated exterior MF glass towels only.
chems: OR Autofinesse Crystal — RTU (~3 sprays per panel)

normal.title: Clean interior glass — BH Trace-Less or Autofinesse Crystal + Reach & Clean Tool
normal.desc: Use either BH Trace-Less or Autofinesse Crystal. Spray onto MF head of Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool — NOT directly onto glass. First pass damp to clean; swap to fresh head for dry buff pass. Use separate dedicated interior MF glass towels for side windows. Only use lint-free cloths free of detergents and fabric conditioners — these cause smearing.
chems: OR Autofinesse Crystal — RTU (onto tool head)

normal.title: Apply CarPro Darkside to tire sidewalls
normal.desc: Apply DarkSide RTU to clean, 100% DRY sidewall using dedicated foam applicator pad or Darkside Applicator Brush — squirt onto applicator, not directly on tire. Spread evenly. Wipe off any excess. IMPORTANT: Allow minimum 1–2 hours dry time before driving the vehicle. Use in a ventilated area — contains citral, PPE recommended. Wipe any overspray off painted wheel faces immediately.
chems: CarPro Darkside — RTU, squirt onto applicator. MIN 1–2 hrs dry before driving.

normal.title: Dress exterior trim & rubber seals
normal.desc: Apply trim dressing RTU to all exterior black trim, door seals, and weatherstripping with soft trim brush.

normal.title: Final inspection pass
normal.desc: Walk car in direct sunlight at raking angle. ION Green metallic is unforgiving of water spots, missed panels, or glass haze. Spot-correct with QD and a clean MF towel.

maintOnly.title: Bilt Hamber Touchless pre-soak — maint. step 1
maintOnly.desc: One-time calibration: fill IK E Foam Pro 12 with 6L water, discharge into bucket, measure total output volume. Multiply by 0.01–0.015 to get ml of Touchless needed (typically 60–90ml for 6L at 1–1.5% PIR). Apply thick coat top to bottom. Dwell 1–5 min. Do NOT let dry. Rinse completely before Descale.
chems: Bilt Hamber Touchless — 60–90ml per 6L (1–1.5% PIR — calibrate to your unit)

maintOnly.title: CarPro Descale by hand — maint. step 2
maintOnly.desc: Dilute 1:100 — 10ml Descale per 1L water in Bucket 2. Use the 2-bucket method with Bucket 3 as the rinse bucket. Reset is not used in the maintenance wash — Descale replaces it for the contact wash stage. Work one panel at a time, top to bottom. Dwell as long as possible without letting it dry. Rinse each panel completely before moving to the next. ION Green makes mineral hazing very visible — inspect carefully as you go.
chems: CarPro Descale — 1:100 (10ml per 1L) in Bucket 2 — replaces Reset in maint. wash

maintOnly.title: Clean door jambs — APC
maintOnly.desc: Standard step on every maintenance wash. Work one door at a time, door fully open. Pre-rinse with low-pressure hose top to bottom, flushing drain holes. Spray AllClean (3:1–5:1) or MultiX (10:1) into jamb, dwell 1–2 min. Agitate flat surfaces with a soft detail brush, hinge hardware and striker plate with a stiff brush, rubber seals thoroughly. The G80 B-pillar has exposed wiring — rinse around connectors, not directly at them. Rinse completely top to bottom, flush drain holes fully. Blow dry with leaf blower into hinges, drain holes, and seal channel. MF towel on flat surfaces. Dress rubber seals and inner seal lip with trim dressing after drying.

engineBay.note: Optional — standalone session only. Engine must be FULLY COOL (2-3 hours minimum after driving). Do not combine with an active wash day — bay needs time to dry fully before driving. Shade preferred, ambient temp below 80°F ideally.

bagAndTape: Fuse box / PDU (driver's side rear of bay)

steps.title: Confirm engine is fully cool
steps.desc: Engine off for at least 2–3 hours. Touch the valve cover and intake manifold — both must be at ambient temperature. A cool engine is non-negotiable on the S58.

steps.title: Cover sensitive components
steps.desc: Bag and tape: alternator, fuse box/PDU, air intake snorkel and filter housing, throttle body area, all exposed wiring harness connectors you can reach. Do not skip — water intrusion into the PDU or alternator is an expensive repair on the G80.

steps.title: Pre-rinse with low-pressure hose
steps.desc: Hose only — NEVER a pressure washer directly in the engine bay. Rinse from back of bay toward front, keeping water away from the firewall wiring looms. Wet all surfaces to prevent APC from drying.

steps.title: Apply APC — AllClean or MultiX
steps.desc: Load AllClean (5:1–10:1) or MultiX (10:1) into IK E Foam Pro 2 or labeled spray bottle. Apply top to bottom across all plastic covers, hoses, bay walls, chassis surfaces. Strengthen to 3:1 for heavy grease or oil deposits. Dwell 1–2 min. Keep APC off coil packs and any uncovered sensor connectors.
tools: IK E Foam Pro 2 or labeled spray bottle
chems: AllClean 5:1–10:1 or MultiX 10:1 (3:1 for heavy deposits)

steps.title: Agitate with detail brushes
steps.desc: Work through all surfaces with assorted detail brushes — engine covers, hose clamps, brackets, strut brace, bay walls. Softer brush on plastic covers, stiffer on chassis and metal brackets. Stay clear of any areas you couldn't bag.

steps.title: Rinse thoroughly — low-pressure hose
steps.desc: Rinse from back to front again, flushing all APC residue out of the bay. Keep water flow controlled — do not direct stream at covered components. Work section by section.

steps.title: Remove covers — dry with leaf blower
steps.desc: Remove all plastic bags and tape. Immediately blow the entire bay dry — work into every gap, around connectors, under hoses and brackets, along the firewall. Allow bay to air for 10–15 min after blowing before considering the job done.

steps.title: Dress plastics, hoses & rubber
steps.desc: Apply trim dressing RTU to all plastic covers, hoses, and rubber seals in the bay with a soft trim brush or foam applicator. Apply thin — do not over-saturate near hot surfaces or connectors. Buff off any excess with a clean MF towel.
tools: Soft trim brush or foam applicator

tools.name: Microfiber wash mitts (long-pile, paint)
tools.why: 2+ needed for paint buckets. One drops mid-wash, you need a backup. Not confirmed in inventory.

tools.name: Dedicated wheel wash mitt or sponge
tools.why: Bucket 1 (WHEELS) needs its own applicator — completely separate from paint mitts.

tools.name: Dedicated MF towels — bug pre-treatment (labeled)
tools.why: Bug Out wipe towels must stay separate from all paint and wash towels. 2 minimum, labeled.

tools.name: Dedicated MF towels — tar pre-treatment (labeled)
tools.why: Tar X towels carry solvent-dissolved tar and must never contact paint mitts or drying towels.

tools.name: Plastic bags + painter's tape (engine bay)
tools.why: Required to cover alternator, fuse box, intake, connectors before any wet engine bay wash.

tools.name: Foam or MF applicator pads — Descale hand application
tools.why: 2–3 pads needed to apply Descale 1:100 by hand panel by panel on maint. wash.

tools.name: Measuring cup / graduated cylinder
tools.why: Reset (~1:50 in IK sprayer), Descale (1:100), and BH Touchless (PIR%) all need accurate measurement.

tools.name: Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool — extra pad heads (2-4 total)
tools.why: Two-pass technique (wet clean + dry buff) needs at least 2 heads per session.

tools.name: Bucket labels (permanent, waterproof)
tools.why: Three buckets need clear permanent labeling: WHEELS / WASH / RINSE.

tools.name: Tire dressing shield / applicator guard
tools.why: Keeps Darkside off the painted wheel face during sidewall application.

tools.name: Small stiff detail brush for exhaust outlet lip
tools.why: Gets inside the exhaust outlet lip where the MF cloth can't reach during P21S cleaning.

tools.name: Dedicated screen MF cloth (ultra-soft, screen-only)
tools.why: Required for iDrive screen cleaning. Must be ultra-soft, lint-free, and used exclusively on screens — never on paint, glass, or any other surface.

tools.name: Soft leather brush
tools.why: For Colourlock Leather Mild Cleaner application — gentler than a MF applicator on perforated or textured leather. Not confirmed in inventory.

tools.name: Stiff carpet brush
tools.why: Needed for carpet and TuxMat agitation. A dedicated stiff-bristle brush for carpet/fabric — not a wheel brush.

tools.name: Bissell 3624 Spot Clean Professional
tools.why: Confirmed carpet extractor for use with Adam's Polishes Carpet Cleaner. Add if not yet purchased.

chemicals.name: Quick detailer / spot detailer (QD)
chemicals.why: Required for final inspection pass. Not yet confirmed. CarPro Reload or Gyeon Q²M Cure recommended — both coating-safe.

chemicals.name: Plastic / rubber trim dressing (specific product)
chemicals.why: Used every wash and in engine bay but no specific product confirmed. 303 Aerospace, CarPro PERL, or similar water-based dressing.

chemicals.name: Between-wash ceramic maintenance spray
chemicals.why: For ION Green between bi-weekly washes — bird drops, tree sap, light road film. Gyeon Q²M Cure or CarPro ELIXIR.

chemicals.name: CarPro HydrO2 Foam (ceramic SiO2 topper)
chemicals.why: Slotted into maint. wash as touchless coating topper. Applied via IK E Foam Pro 12, flashes and blows off with leaf blower.

chemicals.name: APC for door jambs (separate labeled bottle)
chemicals.why: AllClean and MultiX confirmed for wheel wells and engine bay. Door jambs benefit from their own dedicated bottle at 3:1–5:1.

chemicals.name: Wheel coating / sealant (one-time application)
chemicals.why: CarPro CQuartz Wheel or Gtechniq C5 Wheel Armour. Reduces how hard MWC has to work each wash. Best applied at next maint. wash.

chemicals.name: Koch Chemie Speed Glass (GC)
chemicals.why: Real Koch Chemie product — non-alcohol based, confirmed safe on iDrive screens, piano black, and glossy surfaces. Use for iDrive display cleaning.

chemicals.name: Gtechniq C6 or CarPro Carbon C2 (carbon fibre protectant)
chemicals.why: For the factory interior CF trim panels. Protects gloss CF weave and maintains finish. Not needed on open-pore matte CF. Not confirmed in inventory.

chemicals.name: Koch Chemie Textil Star or CarPro Carpro (carpet cleaner)
chemicals.why: Dedicated carpet/fabric cleaner for deeper cleaning than APC alone. Surfex HD covers light carpet work but a dedicated product handles heavier soiling better.

interiorDetail.note: Optional — as needed. Complete interior detail. Work with all doors and windows open in shade. Car off, screens blanked (hold volume roller button with car off).

chemicals.name: nextzett Cockpit Premium
chemicals.usedOn: Dash, door cards, plastic trim, centre console — light maintenance between full details

dilutions.note: Spray onto MF towel, not directly onto surface. Wipe in straight lines. Do not use on screens or carbon fibre.

chemicals.tool: Dedicated interior MF towel

chemicals.name: Colourlock Leather Mild Cleaner
chemicals.usedOn: All leather surfaces — seats, bolsters, steering wheel, door cards, gear selector

dilutions.amount: Dispense foam onto sponge or leather cleaning brush. Do NOT apply directly to leather.
dilutions.note: Apply cleaning foam to sponge or brush — never directly to leather. Clean from seam to seam using circular motions. For ingrained dirt use leather cleaning brush with circular motions seam to seam. Remove dirty foam residue with slightly moist clean cloth. Let leather dry completely before conditioning. Always test on hidden area first.

chemicals.tool: Foam dispenser bottle onto soft sponge or Colourlock Leather Cleaning Brush; clean damp cloth to remove residue

chemicals.name: Colourlock Leather Conditioner
chemicals.usedOn: All leather surfaces after cleaning — restores suppleness and UV protection

dilutions.context: All leather surfaces after cleaning — per kcxusa.com
dilutions.amount: Thin even layer with soft cloth or sponge
dilutions.note: Apply as soon as leather has dried after cleaning, ideally the following day. Shake bottle well. Apply thin even layer. Leave to react — repolish where necessary. One application sufficient. Strained/sunlit areas: treat every 3 months. Contains antioxidants to stop leather decay and UV filters. Do not over-apply.

chemicals.tool: Soft cloth or sponge — thin coat only, buff if needed

chemicals.name: Bilt Hamber Surfex HD (APC)
chemicals.usedOn: Door jambs, floor mats, carpet, plastic door cards, boot/trunk lining

dilutions.note: Spray on, agitate with stiff carpet brush, extract or blot with clean MF towel. Air dry fully.
Spray onto brush or towel, wipe surfaces, follow with a dry MF wipe.
Spray on, wipe with stiff brush or MF, rinse if possible or wipe with damp MF.

chemicals.tool: Labeled spray bottle + appropriate brush + MF towels

chemicals.name: Koch Chemie Speed Glass (GC)
chemicals.usedOn: iDrive curved display, instrument cluster screen, head-up display housing — alcohol-free, anti-static

dilutions.note: BLANK SCREENS FIRST: with car off, press and hold the volume roller button until both iDrive screens go completely dark — prevents accidental inputs during cleaning. Spray 1–2 pumps onto a dedicated ultra-soft screen MF cloth — NEVER spray directly onto screen. Wipe gently in straight lines with minimal pressure. Buff with dry side of cloth. Koch Chemie confirms Speed Glass/GC is safe on screens, piano black, and glossy surfaces. Non-alcohol based — safe on iDrive oleophobic coating. Do NOT use BH Trace-Less or Bilt Hamber Trace-Less on the iDrive display.

chemicals.tool: Dedicated screen MF cloth (ultra-soft, lint-free) — never used on any other surface

chemicals.name: Gtechniq C6 Carbon Fibre or CarPro Carbon C2
chemicals.usedOn: All exposed interior carbon fibre trim panels — dash, centre console, door cards (gloss or open-pore matte)

dilutions.note: Your G80 M3 factory interior carbon fibre trim is GLOSS finish. Clean with Surfex HD (2%) on a soft brush along the weave direction. Wipe clean with soft MF towel. Apply a thin coat of Gtechniq C6 or CarPro Carbon C2 with MF applicator to protect and add depth. Buff off excess. Do not use Cockpit Premium, silicone-based products, or any matte/satin dressings on gloss CF.

chemicals.tool: Soft detail brush (cleaning) + MF applicator pad (protection)

chemicals.name: Adam's Polishes Carpet Cleaner
chemicals.usedOn: Carpet, boot/trunk lining, fabric headliner (if needed) — used with Bissell 3624 Spot Clean Professional for extraction

dilutions.amount: Follow Adam's dilution instructions for the Bissell tank — typically diluted per product directions
dilutions.note: Spray Adam's Carpet Cleaner onto carpet section, agitate with stiff carpet brush in straight lines, then extract with Bissell 3624. Repeat passes until water runs clear. Allow to dry fully with doors open — 1–2 hours minimum. Do not close car until fully dry.

chemicals.tool: Bissell 3624 Spot Clean Professional + stiff carpet brush

steps.title: Prepare — open all doors and windows, blank screens
steps.desc: Park in shade. Open all four doors and all windows for ventilation. Turn car off. BLANK THE SCREENS: press and hold the volume roller button until both iDrive screens go completely dark — do this before any cleaning. Have all products, towels and brushes ready before starting.

steps.title: Vacuum — full interior including TuxMats
steps.desc: Remove TuxMats first. Vacuum all carpet, seats, door card pockets, boot floor, and under seats. Use crevice tool for seat rails, console gaps, and door pockets. Use a soft brush attachment on seats and door cards to avoid scratching. Vacuum TuxMats separately.
tools: Detail brushes (to loosen debris from gaps)

steps.title: TuxMat wipe-down
steps.desc: Spray Surfex HD at 2–3% onto TuxMats. Agitate with a stiff brush. Wipe clean with MF towel or rinse if outside. Allow to dry before reinstalling.

steps.title: Carpet — clean as needed
steps.desc: Spray Adam's Polishes Carpet Cleaner onto carpet sections. Agitate with stiff carpet brush in straight lines — do not scrub in circles. Extract with Bissell 3624 Spot Clean Professional. Run repeated passes until the extracted water runs clear. Allow to dry fully with doors open — minimum 1–2 hours before closing the car.
chems: Adam's Polishes Carpet Cleaner — per label dilution for Bissell 3624

steps.title: Headliner — spot clean if needed
steps.desc: Spot clean only — do not saturate. Spray Surfex HD at 1–2% onto a MF cloth and blot gently — never rub or scrub. Work from outside of stain inward. Allow to air dry fully.
chems: Surfex HD 1–2% on cloth — not sprayed direct

steps.title: Clean iDrive curved display and instrument cluster
steps.desc: BLANK SCREENS FIRST: with car off, press and hold the volume roller button until both screens go completely dark. Spray 1–2 pumps of Koch Chemie Speed Glass (GC) onto a dedicated ultra-soft screen MF cloth — NEVER spray directly onto screen. Wipe gently in straight lines with minimal pressure. Buff immediately with dry side of cloth. Do NOT use BH Trace-Less, Bilt Hamber Trace-Less, or any alcohol or glass cleaner on the iDrive display.
chems: Koch Chemie Speed Glass (GC) — RTU, onto cloth only. NEVER spray direct on screen.

steps.title: Clean plastic trim and dash
steps.desc: Spray Surfex HD (2–3%) or Cockpit Premium onto a MF towel. Wipe all plastic surfaces — dash, centre console, door cards, vents. Use detail brushes on vent slats and button edges. Follow with a dry MF wipe. Do not use Cockpit Premium on screens or carbon fibre trim.
chems: Surfex HD 2–3% or Cockpit Premium — RTU (on cloth)

steps.title: Clean interior carbon fibre trim
steps.desc: Spray Surfex HD (2%) onto a soft detail brush. Gently agitate the CF trim panels — work along the weave direction. Wipe clean with a soft MF towel. For GLOSS CF: apply a thin coat of Gtechniq C6 or CarPro Carbon C2 with MF applicator, buff off. For MATTE/open-pore CF: dry wipe only after cleaning — no dressings or coating products.
chems: Gtechniq C6 or CarPro Carbon C2 — RTU (gloss CF only)

steps.title: Clean leather — all surfaces
steps.desc: Apply Colourlock Leather Mild Cleaner to a soft leather brush or MF applicator — not directly onto leather. Work in circular motions on all leather surfaces: seats, bolsters, steering wheel (avoid airbag seam), door cards, gear selector. Wipe off with clean MF towel. Do not saturate — multiple light passes better than one heavy soak. Allow leather to dry before conditioning.
tools: Soft leather brush or MF applicator
chems: Colourlock Leather Mild Cleaner — RTU or 1:1 dilution

steps.title: Condition leather — all surfaces
steps.desc: Apply Colourlock Leather Conditioner to a MF applicator in a thin even coat. Work into all leather surfaces in circular motion. Allow to absorb 5–10 min. Buff off any excess with a clean dry MF towel. Do not over-apply — leather should feel supple, not greasy.
chems: Colourlock Leather Conditioner — RTU, thin coat

steps.title: Interior glass — BH Trace-Less or Autofinesse Crystal
steps.desc: Use BH Trace-Less or Autofinesse Crystal. Spray onto MF head of Invisible Glass Reach & Clean Tool — NOT directly onto glass. First pass damp to clean; swap to fresh suede/waffle head for dry buff pass. Use separate dedicated interior MF glass towels for side windows. Only use clean lint-free cloths free of detergents and fabric conditioners.
chems: OR Autofinesse Crystal — RTU (onto tool head)

steps.title: Dress interior trim (optional)
steps.desc: Apply a light coat of interior trim dressing or Cockpit Premium to plastic trim and rubber seals if desired. Avoid gloss finish products — use matte/satin dressings to match the BMW interior's OEM finish. Do not dress steering wheel, pedals, gear selector, or any grip surface.
tools: MF applicator pad or soft trim brush
chems: Cockpit Premium or interior trim dressing — RTU (on cloth)

steps.title: Reinstall TuxMats — final check
steps.desc: Ensure TuxMats are fully dry before reinstalling. Walk through the interior with a flashlight at low angle — check for missed spots, streaks on screens, and any damp areas. Leave doors open until fully dry.
